topic.what: Discovery that substituted benzimidazoles inhibit gastric acid secretion by blocking the H+/K+ ATPase (proton pump) in parietal cells
topic.how: In-vitro studies using isolated gastric glands and microsomal preparations to demonstrate that benzimidazole compounds specifically and irreversibly inhibit the H+/K+ ATPase enzyme responsible for the final step of acid secretion
topic.why_matters: Foundational discovery that launched the proton pump inhibitor drug class, which became the most effective acid-suppressive therapy and a cornerstone of gastritis, peptic ulcer, and GORD treatment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Selectivity of benzimidazole inhibition for H+/K+ ATPase over other ATPases
characteristics.value: highly specific for gastric H+/K+ ATPase; minimal effect on Na+/K+ ATPase
characteristics.vs_baseline: distinct mechanism from H2-receptor antagonists which act upstream on histamine signalling
characteristics.context: Isolated parietal cell and microsomal preparations

good_for: rational drug design for acid suppression
requires: understanding of gastric parietal cell physiology

novelty: First identification of H+/K+ ATPase as the molecular target of benzimidazole-mediated acid suppression
Established the proton pump as a druggable target, enabling development of omeprazole and subsequent PPIs
open_problems: In-vivo pharmacokinetics and acid-dependent activation mechanism not yet characterised at time of publication
Reversibility and duration of inhibition in living systems required further study
Clinical efficacy and safety profile remained to be established
